title: GET /og/share/:id — dynamic OG card for the share-link surface
problem: When someone shares a /share/:id URL on Slack/iMessage/LinkedIn/etc, the unfurl preview needs a personalised card — project title, tagline, tier, status — instead of a generic site image. Generated at edge-request time via workers-og (satori + resvg WASM) so the card reflects the live session state without a pre-render step. Falls back to the static /og-image.png if anything in the render path fails — the share URL still works, the preview just isn't personalised.
analysis: GET only, unauth. loadSession by :id; if missing or not showcased, fall back to a 302 → /og-image.png. Otherwise: build OgFields { title, tagline, tier, status } from the session row, load fonts via loadOgFonts (functions/_lib/og-fonts.ts), render through workers-og's ImageResponse. PNG output is 1200×630 (the OG spec sweet spot Slack/iMessage prefer over SVG). Aggressive edge caching headers since the underlying showcase title rarely changes. ?debug=1 returns the resolved fields as text/plain JSON — useful for verifying reachability without bootstrapping satori. captureWorkerException on errors so Sentry sees rendering failures; fallback still serves so the visitor experience never breaks.

decisions.rationale: Slack and iMessage's OG preview implementations explicitly refuse SVG (Twitter accepts it). PNG works everywhere. The cost is the resvg WASM render step at the edge, which adds ~150ms per cold cache hit; cached responses are instant.
decisions.recommendation: Don't switch to SVG. If a future fix wants smaller payloads, evaluate WebP — both Slack and iMessage accept it.

decisions.decision: Fall back to static /og-image.png on ANY render failure
decisions.rationale: A failed dynamic OG card shouldn't break the share UX. The static fallback is generic but works; the share URL still resolves to the real /share/:id page. captureWorkerException reports to Sentry so the failure is visible operationally, not silent.
decisions.recommendation: Keep the fallback. If failures spike, fix the root cause (font loading, satori bug); don't ever 500 to the unfurl scraper.

decisions.decision: ?debug=1 returns plain text instead of PNG
decisions.rationale: When debugging an unreachable or misconfigured endpoint, the FIRST thing to verify is 'is the handler reached at all?'. A debug mode that returns the resolved fields as text lets a curl distinguish 'function is firing, just rendering wrong' from 'function never reached'. Same shape mirrored in /og/home and /og/certificate/:id.
decisions.recommendation: Don't remove the debug mode. It's how RUNBOOK § OG triage starts.

successCriteria:
- A scraper requesting /og/share/<live-id> gets a personalised PNG within ~150ms cold
- A missing/unshowcased session falls back to the static /og-image.png
- ?debug=1 returns the resolved fields as JSON
- Render exceptions are captured to Sentry without breaking the preview UX
- PNG dimensions are 1200×630
implementation: functions/og/share/[id].ts — single onRequestGet. workers-og's ImageResponse handles the satori → resvg pipeline. OgFields interface mirrors the consumer-side projection. Fonts loaded once per render via loadOgFonts (in-memory cache; functions/_lib/og-fonts.ts handles the cold-load). Tests: functions/og/share/[id].test.ts covers happy + missing + debug branches.
userGuide: Used by social scrapers via the <meta property='og:image'> tag in /share/:id's HTMLRewriter-injected head. To preview manually: visit /og/share/<id> in a browser (renders PNG inline). To debug: append ?debug=1 to see the resolved fields. To force a re-render after a session title change: invalidate edge cache via CF dashboard, or wait for natural TTL.
knownLimitations: satori font support is limited; new font files must go in functions/_lib/og-fonts.ts and be loadable as TTF/OTF
PNG render adds ~150ms to cold-cache hits; warmed cache is instant
Fallback to static og-image.png means a broken render is silent to the user; Sentry capture is the only signal
The id is the session id; a typo in the URL falls back gracefully — no leak of session existence
Edge cache TTL is set generously; a rapid title change won't propagate immediately to social scrapers

statusHistory.reason: Endpoint shipped with workers-og pipeline + static fallback + debug mode.
Code-first doc co-located. Render-with-fallback pattern stable.
